process_name: Lepton-flavor-violating Higgs decay h -> e mu
implementation_difficulty_reason: The direct experimental limit is simple to catalog, but production-grade RS integration needs a new charged-lepton Higgs-Yukawa misalignment calculation, a convention for Y_e_mu/Y_mu_e and their relation to B(h -> e mu), and a Higgs production/total-width interpretation. The current code only has diagonal charged-lepton Yukawas and a mu -> e gamma dipole filter.

evidence: Targeted grep for h -> e mu / H -> e mu / Y_e_mu / Y_mu_e across the required code directories returned no hits.
The existing charged-lepton LFV implementation is mu -> e gamma only: flavorConstraints/muToEGamma.py:1 and flavorConstraints/muToEGamma.py:75.
The scan applies the mu -> e gamma LFV filter at scanParams/scan.py:523 and scanParams/scan.py:524; no h -> e mu branch or Higgs LFV limit is present.
Charged-lepton Yukawa support is a diagonal mass-relation helper at yukawa/charged_lepton.py:1 and yukawa/charged_lepton.py:36, not a Higgs LFV observable.
grep_commands: rg -n "epsilon_K|epsilon_k|Delta m_K|DELTA_M_K|evaluate_delta_mk|B_d|b_d|B_s|b_s|D0|d_mix|mu.?e.?gamma|muToEGamma|BR_LIMIT|MEG" quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g "!*.ipynb"

rg -n "b.?->.?s|X_s|K\\*|R_K|R_D|B_s.*mu|pi.*nu|nu.*nubar|epsilon.?prime|eps.?prime|t.?->|Z.?->|h.?->|H.?->|higgs|Higgs|LFV|lepton.?flavou?r|EDM|dipole|conversion|tau.?->|mu.?->.?3e" quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g "!*.ipynb"

rg -n "h\\s*[-=]>\\s*e\\s*mu|H\\s*[-=]>\\s*e\\s*mu|Higgs.*e\\s*mu|LFV.*Higgs|Higgs.*LFV|Y_e_mu|Y_mu_e|Yemu|emu" quarkConstraints qcd flavorConstraints neutrinos yukawa warpConfig solvers scanParams tests -g "!*.ipynb"

Writer polish completed for mixed charged-lepton/top-Higgs batch; numerical claims checked against YAML source keys.

issues: CHK-1: T020.tex quotes the 125 GeV Higgs mass hypothesis, but YAML pdg_or_equivalent has it only in value_summary/snapshots, not as a per-value entry with year, value, source URL, access date, and sha256.

Cycle-2 checker re-check passed CHK-1 through CHK-8 after WA-v2 metadata fixes.
Opus round 2 per-process sign-off; CA-verified PASS retained; see flavor_catalog/signoff/round_002_index.md.

status_history.notes: Mismatch: ATLAS h -> e mu primary source gives observed limit < 6.2 x 10^-5, not < 6.1 x 10^-5.

status_history.note: ATLAS h -> e mu limit: 6.1e-5 -> 6.2e-5 per ATLAS Run-2 / PDG source

sections.process:
\textbf{Standard notation:} \(\mathcal{B}(h\to e^\pm\mu^\mp)\).
This entry catalogs the lepton-flavor-violating Higgs decay \(h\to e\mu\)
in the top/Higgs/electroweak family.  It covers direct ATLAS and CMS searches
for a \(125~\mathrm{GeV}\) Higgs boson decaying to an opposite-sign
electron--muon pair (\texttt{PDG2025HiggsLFVReview}).
sections.pdg_prose:
The source-of-truth values are in \texttt{T020.yaml:pdg\_or\_equivalent}.  The
PDG 2025 Higgs review summarizes the full \(\sqrt{s}=13~\mathrm{TeV}\) Run-2
direct limits on LFV Higgs decays (\texttt{PDG2025HiggsLFVReview}).  For
\(h\to e\mu\), PDG quotes
\[
  \mathcal{B}(h\to e\mu) < 4.4\times 10^{-5}
  \quad (4.7\times 10^{-5}\ \mathrm{expected})
\]
from CMS at \(95\%\) CL using \(138~\mathrm{fb}^{-1}\)
(\texttt{CMS2023HiggsLFVEMu}; \texttt{PDG2025:T020:cms\_run2}).  PDG also
quotes the ATLAS limit
\[
  \mathcal{B}(h\to e\mu) < 6.2\times 10^{-5}
  \quad (5.9\times 10^{-5}\ \mathrm{expected})
\]
at \(95\%\) CL (\texttt{PDG2025:T020:atlas\_run2}).  The primary ATLAS abstract
reports the same search as \(6.2\times10^{-5}\) observed and
\(5.9\times10^{-5}\) expected with \(139~\mathrm{fb}^{-1}\)
(\texttt{ATLAS2020HiggsEMu}); this agrees with the PDG value kept as the
catalog headline.  The CMS number is the strongest direct single-experiment
limit.
sections.relevance:
Warped or partially composite lepton sectors can generate off-diagonal
physical Higgs Yukawa couplings after the charged-lepton mass matrix is
diagonalized.  The \(h\to e\mu\) search directly probes the \(e\)-\(\mu\)
entries of that Higgs Yukawa matrix and is therefore complementary to the
repo's existing \(\mu\to e\gamma\) dipole filter.  It is not a constraint on
the current quark \(\Delta F=2\) scan by itself; it belongs to a future
lepton-sector extension.
sections.post_2008:
The requested arXiv:0804.1954 baseline predates the LHC Higgs LFV program
(\texttt{CsakiFalkowskiWeiler2008WarpedFlavor}).  Harnik, Kopp, and Zupan
then framed flavor-violating Higgs decays as direct discovery channels and
showed that LFV Higgs branching fractions can be sizable in generic EFT
settings (\texttt{HarnikKoppZupan2012FlavorViolatingHiggs}).  ATLAS produced a
full Run-2 \(h\to e\mu\) limit with
\(139~\mathrm{fb}^{-1}\) (\texttt{ATLAS2020HiggsEMu}).  CMS later used
\(138~\mathrm{fb}^{-1}\), improved the direct Higgs limit to
\(4.4\times10^{-5}\), and extended the same \(e\mu\) mass scan to
\(110\)--\(160~\mathrm{GeV}\).  The largest excess was near
\(146~\mathrm{GeV}\), with local/global significance \(3.8/2.8\sigma\);
CMS did not interpret it as evidence (\texttt{CMS2023HiggsLFVEMu};
\texttt{CMS2023:T020:mass\_scan}).
sections.validity:
Experimentally this is a clean direct upper limit on a Standard-Model-forbidden
Higgs decay.  A literal branching-ratio cut assumes the SM-like Higgs
production mixture and total width used by ATLAS and CMS.  Models with altered
Higgs production, exotic Higgs width, or correlated low-energy LFV signals need
a recast.  PDG notes that indirect \(\mu\to e\gamma\)-based constraints are
stronger for this channel, so this entry should be used as the direct collider
observable rather than the global \(e\)-\(\mu\) LFV bound.
sections.code_coverage_prose:
Coverage is \textbf{NO}.  The required greps and a targeted search for
\texttt{h->e mu}, \texttt{H->e mu}, \texttt{Y\_e\_mu}, and \texttt{Y\_mu\_e}
across \texttt{quarkConstraints/}, \texttt{qcd/}, \texttt{flavorConstraints/},
\texttt{neutrinos/}, \texttt{yukawa/}, \texttt{warpConfig/}, \texttt{solvers/},
\texttt{scanParams/}, and \texttt{tests/} found no observable implementation.
The existing lepton LFV backend is specifically \(\mu\to e\gamma\)
(\texttt{flavorConstraints/muToEGamma.py:1};
\texttt{flavorConstraints/muToEGamma.py:75}), and the scan applies that filter
at \texttt{scanParams/scan.py:523}--\texttt{scanParams/scan.py:524}.  The
charged-lepton Yukawa helper is diagonal
(\texttt{yukawa/charged\_lepton.py:1}; \texttt{yukawa/charged\_lepton.py:36}).
See \texttt{T020.yaml:code\_coverage}.
sections.implementation_difficulty_prose:
Production integration is \textbf{HIGH}.  The direct limit is easy to catalog,
but a scan constraint needs a new charged-lepton Higgs-Yukawa misalignment
calculation, a convention for \(Y_{e\mu}\) and \(Y_{\mu e}\), and a Higgs-rate
or total-width interpretation before accepting or rejecting points
(\texttt{T020.yaml:implementation\_difficulty\_rationale}).
